assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting for foreign currency risk under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intercompany receivable/payable, creating a monetary item.
Relevant group entities may have different functional currencies, so foreign exchange differences may arise in consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk, and the context specifically addresses foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a separate hedge accounting model and the context specifically includes qualifying foreign currency hedges of highly probable forecast intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct foreign currency hedge accounting model applied at the consolidated financial statement level.
